title: rubus schleicheri var. furvus
intro: Rubus schleicheri var. furvus, commonly known as the black raspberry, is a type of plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to eastern North America. The medicinal properties of black raspberry are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of black raspberry include its potential to reduce the risk of certain cancers, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Black raspberry can be prepared as a tea, juice, or supplement, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other food products.
